title: Six to ten — and zero
pedagogy: Vocabulary: หก (high ห + dead ก → low), เจ็ด (mid จ + dead ด → low), แปด (mid ป + dead ด → low), เก้า (mid ก + mai tho → falling), สิบ (high ส + dead บ → low), ศูนย์ (high ศ + live น → rising; ย์ karan). Note the dead-syllable cluster: 6, 7, 8, 10 all land low by rule, from two different classes.

exercises.title: Four numbers, one tone, two reasons
body: หก เจ็ด แปด สิบ all come out low — but not by copying each other. Each one earns it: high class + dead ending, or mid class + dead ending. Both roads lead to low.
เก้า (nine) breaks the pattern with a falling tone — and it's the lucky one, because it sounds like ก้าว, “to step forward”.
ศูนย์ (zero) you'll use most for phone numbers, which Thais read digit by digit.
